creator: wietse@porcupine.org
text:
Short answer:

As in the original problem report, The Solaris 10 guest ignores
keyboard input after booting from install CD into single-user mode.
This does not happen with virtualbox-3.0.51.r22902_1.

To speed up the fix for this regression, I can make an ISO image
of the installation CD available, so that developers don't have to
depend on me for testing their fixes.

Details:

On the same quad-core machine as in the original problem report I
deinstalled/reinstalled virtualbox-ose and -kmod version 3.1.6,
rebooted, and moved the .VirtualBox directory out of the way.

As in the original problem report, I booted from the install CD,
and entered "b -s" in response to the installation type prompt.
As in the original problem report, Solaris 10 came up in single-user
mode, and ignored keyboard input. When I select interactive
installation it also ignores keyboard input.

Below is a copy of the VirtualBox logfile for the single-user boot
(""b -s" response to the installation type prompt).

Additional information:

This is with CPU virtualization support turned off (otherwise the
CD boot loader just hangs while "Running Configuration Assistant".

Both Solaris 9 and 10 work with virtualbox-3.0.51.r22902_1, meaning
that I can boot from their install CD, reconfigure their /devices
directory in a disk image from Vmware 5, and then use the virtual
machine as before.

Now, Solaris 10 hangs on keyboard input, while the Solaris 9 console
boots up into a series of "segmentation fault" messages.  I'll come
back to the problem with Solaris 9 once the regression with Solaris
10 is resolved, as it may also undo the damage for Solaris 9.
